info.title: Pulp API
info.description: Pulp social-listening HTTP API. This spec is generated from the Rust backend and is the authoritative API contract.

post.summary:
Run a single notifier pass synchronously: fan every feed-visible,
un-notified mention out to its workspace's notifications, then mark them
notified. Mirrors the background loop so the pipeline can be driven
deterministically.

get.summary: `GET /api/channels` — list every channel config (global, not workspace-scoped).

`GET /api/channels/:channel` — fetch a single channel config by name.
Returns 404 if the channel has never been configured.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/channels/:channel` — create or update a channel config. Body fields
are all optional: `enabled`, `poll_interval` (seconds, default 900), and
`credentials` (channel-specific JSON). For Reddit the only (optional) credential
is `{ "user_agent" }` — Reddit's public search needs no client id/secret. For
GitHub the credentials are a `{ "token", ... }` filter object. When `enabled` is
omitted the channel's current enabled state is preserved. When `credentials` is
omitted the previously-stored credentials are preserved (not wiped) — pass an
explicit `credentials` object to change them.

get.summary:
`GET /api/channels/{channel}/targets` — per-target collection status for a
channel (watermark, last success/attempt, consecutive failures, last error,
derived health) plus each target's open backfill jobs. Backend-only status view
for the targeted (auto-recovering) collector pipeline.

get.description:
`service` accepts any channel name (`reddit`, `hackernews`, `github`) and
future services (`ai_filter`/`llm`). Unknown services → 404. The number of
lines is capped (default 200, max 2000).

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Service id: a channel name or ai_filter/llm

get.summary:
`GET /api/mentions` — list mentions (keyset-paginated by the compound
`before`/`before_id` cursor).

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
Upper bound (exclusive) on the effective timestamp (`published_at`,
or `ingested_at` when `published_at` is null). For the feed's
keyset-pagination cursor, pair this with `before_id` — the `id` of
the last item on the previous page — so rows tied on the effective
timestamp are still visited exactly once when paging one page at a
time; `before` alone has no tiebreak.

parameters.name: before_id
parameters.description:
Tiebreaker id paired with `before` for keyset pagination — the `id`
of the last item on the previous page. See `before`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound: return mentions published at or after this epoch.

parameters.name: read
parameters.description:
Filter by read state: `true` = read only, `false` = unread only,
omitted = both.

parameters.name: ai
parameters.description:
AI verdict filter: omitted/`visible` = feed default (no verdict or
`accepted`); `all` = everything; `pending` / `accepted` / `rejected`
= exactly that verdict.

get.summary:
`GET /api/mentions/pending-count` — size + age of the AI-filter backlog,
driving the feed's "N pending AI filter" banner.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Scope the count to a single workspace; omitted = all workspaces.

get.summary:
`GET /api/mentions/:id` — fetch a single mention (used by the mention
detail page that web-push notifications deep-link to).

put.summary: `PUT /api/mentions/:id/read` — mark a mention read or unread.

get.summary:
`GET /api/monitors?workspace_id=…` — list monitors in a workspace.
The `workspace_id` query param is required.

post.summary:
`POST /api/monitors` — create a monitor watch. Body is [`CreateMonitor`]:
`workspace_id` and `terms` (the match-any keyword list) are required;
`channels` (empty/omitted = all channels), `exact_match`, `case_sensitive`,
`exclude_terms` are optional.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/monitors/:id` — update a monitor watch. Body is [`UpdateMonitor`];
every field is optional and only provided fields are changed.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/monitors/:id` — delete a monitor watch (cascades to its mentions).

post.summary: `POST /api/workspaces` — create a workspace. Body: `{ name, description? }`.

put.summary: `PUT /api/workspaces/:id` — rename/update a workspace. Body: `{ name, description? }`.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/workspaces/:id` — delete a workspace (cascades to its monitors and notifications).

AiConfigUpdate.description:
Partial update. Every field is optional; omitted (or null) fields are left
unchanged. For `api_key`, an empty string clears the stored key.

AiConfigView.description:
AI-filter settings as exposed to clients. The API key is never returned;
`api_key_set` reports only whether one is configured.

AiTestResult.description: Result of a `test` call: a sample verdict on success, or an error message.

BackfillJob.description:
A durable backfill job: a half-open `[range_start, range_end)` window of one
target that still needs older pages walked. Survives restarts; resumes from
`next_cursor`.

next_cursor.description: The `after` token to resume paging from.

range_end.description: Newer bound — shrinks toward `range_start` as older pages are banked.

BackfillResult.description: Result of a channel backfill: a human-readable status message.

caught_up_at.description:
The caught-up watermark: how far this channel is contiguously
ingested. Distinct from `last_polled_at`, which is merely the last
attempt time.

ChannelHealthSummary.description:
Per-health counts plus a ready-to-display degraded message, summarizing a
channel's targets at time `now`. The `message` is `None` when nothing is
degraded; otherwise it leads with "rate-limited" when any target is throttled
(so the banner reads as a throttle), else "degraded".

degraded.description: Targets that are throttled or failing (the banner count).

message.description: A human-readable degraded summary, or `None` when all targets are fine.

CleanupPreview.description:
Preview returned by a dry-run cleanup: how many mentions would be removed,
plus up to 10 samples.

CleanupResponse.description:
A cleanup call returns a preview on `dry_run` and a delete count otherwise.
Untagged so the wire shape is exactly one of the two inner objects.

CleanupResult.description: Result of a real (non-dry-run) cleanup: how many mentions were deleted.

CollectorTarget.description:
A durable collection target: one consolidated upstream request (an OR-batched
global search, or a per-sub search) that the targeted
runner pages, banks progress for, and tracks sticky failure status on.

confirmed_watermark.description:
Timestamp below which this target has been contiguously ingested. The
channel is "caught up" only when all its targets' watermarks are recent.

descriptor.description: Sub-list or query — human-readable, and the basis for `id`.

id.description: Stable id derived from `channel + kind + descriptor`.

last_error.description:
Sticky: a different target succeeding does NOT clear this. Display/health
only — pacing is governed globally by the adaptive rate limiter, not a
per-target backoff.

ai_filter_prompt.description: AI relevance prompt; empty/whitespace is normalized to "disabled".

channel_settings.description: Per-channel collection overrides keyed by channel name.

terms.description:
Match-any term list (each a bare literal phrase). A post matches if it
contains ANY term and none of `exclude_terms`.

LogResponse.description:
Response body: the resolved service, the lines (oldest → newest), and
whether the underlying log file existed.

ai_reason.description: The judge's one-sentence explanation for the verdict.

ai_verdict.description:
AI filter verdict: `None` = no AI filtering applied, `pending` =
awaiting judgment (hidden from the feed), `accepted` / `rejected`.

read_at.description: When the user marked this mention read; `None` = unread.

MentionPage.description: A page of mentions plus a cursor hint for keyset pagination.

ai_filter_prompt.description:
When set, this monitor's new mentions are held out of the feed until
the AI judge accepts them against this prompt.

channel_settings.description:
Per-channel collection overrides, keyed by channel name (e.g.
`{"reddit": {"subreddits": ["accessibility"]}}`). Shallow-merged over
the channel's global credentials at collection time; monitor keys win.

terms.description:
Match-any keyword/phrase list: a post matches this monitor if it contains
ANY of these terms (per `exact_match` / `case_sensitive`) and none of
`exclude_terms`. Each term is a bare literal phrase — never OR/quotes.

Notification.description:
A per-workspace notification: a pure delivery endpoint. Every mention that
enters the feed fans out to ALL notifications in its workspace — there is no
matching, criteria, per-monitor toggle, or frequency. `kind` selects the
transport and `config` is the kind-specific JSON:
- `webpush`: `{ endpoint, p256dh, auth }` (the browser's subscription)
- `webhook`: `{ url }`

PendingCount.description:
Size and age of the AI-filter backlog for a workspace — how many mentions
are still awaiting a verdict (withheld from the feed) and when the oldest was
ingested. The feed shows a "N pending AI filter" banner once the oldest has
aged past a staleness threshold.

oldest_ingested_at.description:
`ingested_at` (epoch seconds) of the oldest pending mention, or `null`
when nothing is pending.

read.description: `true` marks the mention read, `false` marks it unread.

TargetHealth.description:
Coarse health of one collection target, derived purely from its sticky
status fields. This is the *single source of truth* for both the per-target
status badge (`GET /api/channels/{channel}/targets`) and the channel-level
degraded summary — both call [`target_health`] / [`summarize_targets`], so a
banner count can never drift from the table. It is time-independent: with
throttling governed globally by the rate limiter (no per-target backoff
clock), a target's health depends only on its latest attempt outcome.

health.description:
Coarse health derived from the sticky status fields (the same classifier
the channel-level degraded banner counts), computed live at request time.

open_jobs.description: Open backfill jobs for this target (durable, survive restart).

TargetStatus.description:
Per-channel collection status: every durable target with its watermark and
sticky failure status, plus a summary of its open backfill jobs.

summary.description:
Per-health counts + a degraded banner message, computed from the same
classifier as each target's `health`, so the banner can't disagree with
the per-target list.

oneOf.description:
Live state of the channel's shared rate-limiter lane, if one has been
materialized yet. `None` before the first collection pass and for
simple-poller channels that don't pace through a lane.

TestNotificationResult.description:
Result of sending a test notification: how many of the workspace's
notifications it attempted to deliver to.

ThrottleState.description:
A snapshot of a channel's live adaptive rate-limiter (the shared lane all of
the channel's targets pace through). Surfaced so the UI can watch the
interval converge as the limiter adapts.

interval_secs.description: The current minimum spacing between requests, in seconds.

paused.description:
Whether issuance is currently gated to a future instant (a hard
`Retry-After` pause, or simply the next paced slot).

rate_per_min.description: The current effective issuance rate, in requests per minute (`rate_per_sec * 60`).

ai_filter_prompt.description: AI relevance prompt; pass an empty string to clear.

channel_settings.description: Per-channel collection overrides; pass `{}` to clear.

terms.description: Replace the match-any term list. Omit to leave it unchanged.

VapidPublicKey.description:
The server's VAPID public key (base64url, uncompressed P-256 point) — the
browser needs it as `applicationServerKey` when subscribing.

tags.name: push
tags.description: Web Push VAPID key (browser subscribe bootstrap)
